content.rendered:
Bike Rentals in Santa Monica!

RIDE THE STRAND

Boardwalk Bike Rentals rents bikes for adults and kids just steps from one of the most spectacular bicycle paths in the world.

Reservations

No reservations needed! In most instances, you'll be off and riding in less than 15 minutes.

Security Deposit

A valid photo identification such as a Drivers License or Passport will be held during your journey. A debit or credit card is also required for a pre authorization deposit.

Welcome To
Boardwalk Bike Rentals!

  • 1619 Ocean Front Walk • Santa Monica, CA 90401
Boardwalk Bike Rentals, Inc. is family owned bike rental founded in 2021. We’re located in sunny Santa Monica along the boardwalk just steps away from two beautiful bike paths. Spend a day in the sun riding through iconic locations such as Venice Beach, Manhattan Beach, Hermosa Beach, and Redondo Beach! We offer locks, helmets, and baskets free of charge with rentals as well!

Suggested Reading From Our Blog...

Explore Santa Monica’s “Off-The-Beaten” Path Locations On Your Bike Rental

Testimonials!

FAQs
Where are you located?

Our address is 1619 Ocean Front Walk, Santa Monica, CA 90401. We’re located on the corner of Ocean Front Walk and Moss Ave in the building right next to Big Dean’s Ocean Front Café.

Do we need reservations?
No reservations needed! We invite you to come on down and check us out!
What do I need to rent a bike?
Any person who is renting a bike does need to be over 18 years old and we require a physical Drivers License as a security deposit.
What if I’m not 18 years old?

If you’re a minor under 18 years old you will need an adult 18 years or older with a valid ID to rent a bike for you.

Do you offer helmets?
We do! With each rental we offer helmets in a variety of sizes and locks for the bicycles. Baskets are available upon request!
What kind of equipment do you have available for rent?
We have men and women single speed Beach Cruisers, two person adult tandems, kids bikes both with and without training wheels, kiddie trailers that attach to the adult cruisers, and tag a longs that attach to the adult cruisers.

Points Of Interest

Malibu Pier - 12 miles (19.3 km)

Malibu Pier is a Southern California icon in an area once called the Riviera of America. The historic landmark is located in the heart of California's surf culture. Surfers from around the world come to Surfrider Beach adjacent to Malibu Pier, known for its three-point break that offers rides of 300 yards or more.

Source - CA.gov

Pacific Palisades (formerly known as The Long Wharf - 2.4 miles (3.9 km)

The Long Wharf in Santa Monica, also known as Port Los Angeles or the Mile Long Pier, was an extensive pier wharf constructed by the Southern Pacific Railroad Company in Santa Monica Bay from 1892–94. The current site of the Long Wharf is now Pacific Palisades and the California State Route 1, at the Will Rogers State Beach lifeguard headquarters.

Source - Wikipedia

Venice Boardwalk - 3 miles (4.8 km)

Venice Beach Boardwalk, originally named “Venice of America” is located in sunny Los Angeles and runs almost over two miles long along the coast of Southern California. The Boardwalk first started out as a seaside resort founded in 1905 by tobacco millionaire, Abbot Kinney.

Source - Local Wiki

Marina del Rey - 3.2 miles (5.1 km)

Marina del Rey is an unincorporated seaside community in Los Angeles County, California, with an eponymous harbor that is a major boating and water recreation destination of the greater Los Angeles area.

Source - Wikipedia

Manhattan Beach Pier - 13 miles (19.3 km)

The Manhattan Beach Pier is a pier located in Manhattan Beach, California, on the coast of the Pacific Ocean. The pier is 928 feet long and located at the end of Manhattan Beach Boulevard.

Source - Wikipedia

Hermosa Beach Pier - 14 miles (22.5 km)

The Hermosa Beach pier is located in the Southern California community of Hermosa Beach. It extends into the Pacific Ocean. In 1904 the first pier was built. It was constructed entirely of wood even to the pilings and it extended five hundred feet out into the ocean.

Source - Wikipedia

Redondo Pier/King Harbor - 16 miles (25.7 km)

Redondo Beach is a coastal city in Los Angeles County, California, United States, located in the South Bay region of the Greater Los Angeles area. It is one of three adjacent beach cities along the southern portion of Santa Monica Bay.

Source - Wikipedia

Palos Verdes - 19 miles (30.6 km)

The Palos Verdes Peninsula is a landform and a geographic sub-region of the Los Angeles metropolitan area, within southwestern Los Angeles County.

Source - Wikipedia
